globalRules: The course uses exactly three non-overlapping project roots: community-site for guided learning, volunteer-hub for all module projects, and neighborhood-guide for the capstone.
Every technical guided exercise in Modules 1-3 uses community-site and advances its canonical milestone without introducing a new repository story.
All three module projects use volunteer-hub cumulatively across modules; Module 2 begins from the complete Module 1 project state and Module 3 begins from the complete Module 2 project state.
The six capstone steps use neighborhood-guide cumulatively and never reuse history from the other two journeys.
Every exercise is direct-link and reset safe: hidden setup reconstructs the exact prerequisite milestone, while grading checks only the learner transition.
For every journey, solution milestone N equals starter milestone N+1 across files, commit history, index state, working-tree state, ignored files, and cwd.
Any required file edit includes the exact path, exact text, and exact insertion or replacement location; no exercise grades HTML, CSS, JavaScript, or creative file-authoring knowledge.
The first hands-on guided exercise starts from an ordinary community-site folder and requires git init -b main.
Hidden setup must never appear in prompts, hints, starterCode, solutions, terminal expectations, terminal history, or the visible explorer.
Final learner quiz cards contain no code_input; runtime Try It exercises remain separate from quiz questions.
Every standard module contains exactly one module_project section, and the final module contains exactly one capstone section and one capstone topic.
No exercise requires branches, remotes, hosted collaboration, public network access, or destructive history rewriting.
Quiz distractors stay inside concepts already taught in this course and do not introduce branches, remotes, GitHub, merges, or other later-course vocabulary.

forbidden: commits beyond the initial project workflow
